# In-Room SDR App-Teleport — Bench Setup & Replication

Drama-free replication guide for the protocol69 over-the-air app-teleport bench:
encode a payload → transmit on an ADALM-Pluto → receive on an RTL-SDR across the
room → decode bit-exact. The whole bench is verifiable in one command
([`scripts/smoke-sdr-teleport.sh`](../scripts/smoke-sdr-teleport.sh)).

> **protocol69 admission:** `broadcastSymbol 66 XOR localFoilOperand 7 = 69`
> (XOR, **not** `66 × 7`). 66 = the RF-key packet `prefixHash`; 7 = its
> `prefixLen`. See `distributed-inference/src/protocol69.rs`.

---

## 1. Bill of materials & device markers

| Role | Device | USB VID:PID | Driver | Markers |
|------|--------|-------------|--------|---------|
| **RX** | Nooelec **NESDR SMArt v5** ×2 | `0bda:2838` (vendor "Nooelec") | librtlsdr / `rtl_test` | RTL2832U + R820T tuner, **RX-only**. Reference serials `78922015`, `94092559` (distinct → individually addressable). |
| **TX** | **ADALM-Pluto Rev.C** ×1 | `0456:b673` ("Analog Devices Inc.") | libiio / `iio_*` | Z7010-AD9361, **TX+RX**. `hw_model: Analog Devices PlutoSDR Rev.C (Z7010-AD9361)`. libiio URI `usb:X.Y.Z` **varies per enumeration → auto-discovered**. |

The Pluto, on a **data** cable, also exposes: a `PlutoSDR` mass-storage volume,
a `/dev/cu.usbmodem*` serial port, and (on Linux/sometimes) a `192.168.2.x`
USB-Ethernet gadget. On macOS the network gadget often does **not** appear
(RNDIS unsupported) — that's fine, libiio talks over the raw USB backend.

**Ground truth for enumeration is `ioreg -p IOUSB`.** `system_profiler
SPUSBDataType` returns 0 bytes on Apple Silicon — don't rely on it.

---

## 2. Toolchain (one-time)

```bash
# RTL-SDR receiver tools
brew install rtl-sdr                     # provides rtl_test, rtl_sdr

# libiio v0.25 (Pluto TX) — ephemeral build under /tmp, fail-closed if gone
open-source/gnosis/scripts/setup-sdr-toolchain.sh --build
#   builds /tmp/libiio_build/build/tests/{iio_info,iio_attr,iio_writedev}

# (optional) gnosis-polyglot — only for the "real runtime" app-cache-key teleport
#   open-source/gnosis/polyglot/target/release/gnosis-polyglot   (cargo build --release)
```

Export the libiio framework path for any direct `iio_*` calls:

```bash
export DYLD_FALLBACK_FRAMEWORK_PATH=/tmp/libiio_build/build
```

`setup-sdr-toolchain.sh --check --probe-hardware` prints toolchain + auto-discovered
Pluto URI. `--build` is idempotent and restores the ephemeral `/tmp` build.

---

## 3. Cabling — read this first (it's always the cable)

The entire "Pluto won't enumerate" failure class is **physical**:

1. **Charge-only USB cable** — powers the device (LED1 solid green) but carries
   **no data**, so it never enumerates. #1 cause, visually identical to a data
   cable. Use a cable you've actually transferred files with.
2. **Wrong Pluto jack** — the Pluto has two USB jacks: **data/host** (outer, →
   the Mac) and **power-only** (inner, → your power block). A data cable in the
   power jack does nothing for the host.
3. **Unpowered / oversubscribed hub** — a second RTL + a Pluto draw real current;
   put them on a **powered** hub or direct Mac ports.

A Pluto on a good data cable mounts the `PlutoSDR` volume within seconds. No
volume + no `/dev/cu.usbmodem` + no `iio_info -s` context = no data link → swap
the cable. Don't chase software.

---

## 4. Smoke test (the drama-free path)

```bash
# Preflight: toolchain + enumeration + marker table. No RF radiated.
open-source/gnosis/scripts/smoke-sdr-teleport.sh            # or --preflight

# Full over-the-air loopback: encodes, RADIATES on 433.92 MHz, captures, decodes,
# and asserts the payload round-tripped bit-exact.
open-source/gnosis/scripts/smoke-sdr-teleport.sh --teleport
```

Expected `--teleport` tail:

```
ok=true matches=true sync=0.999 rsErrors=0
recovered: "GNOSIS-SMOKE/1 teleported across the room p69=66xor7=69"
[ ok ] TELEPORT PASS — payload round-tripped over the air, bit-exact
```

Override anything via env (defaults shown):

```
PLUTO_URI auto / GNOSIS_PLUTO_URI=usb:1.5.5   # pin instead of auto-discover
EXPECTED_RTL_COUNT=2   EXPECTED_RTL_SERIALS="78922015 94092559"
TX_LO_HZ=433920000   SAMPLE_RATE=2400000   TX_GAIN_DB=-10
RX_DEVICE=0   RX_TUNER_GAIN=30   CAPTURE_SECONDS=3
PAYLOAD="...your text..."
```

---

## 5. Manual teleport loop (what the smoke script automates)

```bash
cd open-source/gnosis
export DYLD_FALLBACK_FRAMEWORK_PATH=/tmp/libiio_build/build
URI=usb:1.5.5   # from: iio_info -s   (or setup-sdr-toolchain.sh --probe-hardware)
A=/tmp/libiio_build/build/tests/iio_attr
W=/tmp/libiio_build/build/tests/iio_writedev

# 1. Encode payload -> int16 IQ (note frameSamples in the JSON for -b below)
GNOSIS_PLUTO_URI=$URI node ./bin/rtlsdr-mock-sim.js mars-tx \
  --payload "hello over the air" --tx-lo-hz 433920000 \
  --iq-int16-out .qa-artifacts/rtlsdr/tx.iq16

# 2. Configure the Pluto TX chain (hardwaregain write may print EMULTIHOP(95)
#    over USB but still takes — confirm by readback, retry 2-3x)
"$A" -u $URI -c ad9361-phy altvoltage1 frequency 433920000
"$A" -u $URI -c ad9361-phy voltage0 sampling_frequency 2400000
"$A" -u $URI -c ad9361-phy voltage0 rf_bandwidth 2400000
"$A" -u $URI -c ad9361-phy voltage0 hardwaregain -10

# 3. Cyclic transmit (background) while an RTL captures, then stop TX
"$W" -u $URI -b <frameSamples> -c cf-ad9361-dds-core-lpc voltage0 voltage1 < .qa-artifacts/rtlsdr/tx.iq16 &
rtl_sdr -d 0 -f 433920000 -s 2400000 -g 30 -n 7200000 .qa-artifacts/rtlsdr/rx.u8
kill %1

# 4. Decode + verify
node ./bin/rtlsdr-mock-sim.js mars-rx \
  --iq-file .qa-artifacts/rtlsdr/rx.u8 --payload "hello over the air" \
  --capture-format rtl_u8
```

### Real-runtime variant (teleport a lowered program, not a string)

Generate a protocol69 **app-cache-key** receipt for a real app via the monster
polyglot runner, then teleport that receipt's frame ASCII as the payload:

```bash
node ./bin/rtlsdr-mock-sim.js rf-key --symbol-mode polyglot-app-cache-key \
  --app-source polyglot/tests/fixtures/typed_handler.ts --app-function fetchRecords \
  --polyglot-bin polyglot/target/release/gnosis-polyglot
# -> emits GNOSIS-P69-APP/1 ... cacheKey=gnosis:protocol69:polyglot-app-cache:v1:<hash>
#    feed that .appTeleport.frame.ascii string as the mars-tx --payload above.
```

---

## 6. Troubleshooting (distilled from real bench-bringup)

| Symptom | Cause | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Pluto absent from `ioreg`, LED1 solid green | charge-only cable / wrong jack | data cable in the **data** jack; power block in power jack |
| `iio_info -s` → `No IIO context found` | Pluto not on USB | reseat; verify `PlutoSDR` volume mounts |
| `rtl_test` → `No supported devices found` | RTL on dead cable/hub | direct Mac port, known-data cable |
| RTL count drops when you move things | unpowered/oversubscribed hub | powered hub |
| `hardwaregain` write → `EMULTIHOP (95)` | libiio USB write quirk | benign — value still takes; confirm by readback, retry |
| URI `usb:0.5.5` fails but device present | stale hardcoded URI | it's auto-discovered now; address varies per plug-in |
| `system_profiler SPUSBDataType` empty | Apple-Silicon flake | use `ioreg -p IOUSB` instead |
| TELEPORT FAIL, decode mismatch | weak link / antenna | attach antennas, raise gain toward 0 dB, move RX closer |

---

## 7. Files

- [`scripts/setup-sdr-toolchain.sh`](../scripts/setup-sdr-toolchain.sh) — toolchain check/build + Pluto URI auto-discovery
- [`scripts/smoke-sdr-teleport.sh`](../scripts/smoke-sdr-teleport.sh) — one-command preflight + OTA loopback
- [`rtlsdr-mock-sim/run.mts`](./run.mts) — CLI: `mars-tx`, `mars-rx`, `rf-key`
- [`rtlsdr-mock-sim/pluto-tx.ts`](./pluto-tx.ts) — Pluto IQ + `iio_*` command builders (`resolvePlutoUri()`)
- [`distributed-inference/src/protocol69.rs`](../distributed-inference/src/protocol69.rs) — `66 XOR 7 = 69` constants + rationale
